title: podman doesn't work as non-root user on WSL Ubuntu 20.04
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

podman commands don't work as non-root user on WSL Ubuntu 20.04

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Install WSL ubuntu 20.04 on Windows 10. Install podman. Switch to root and update container.conf with cgroup_manager = "cgroupfs" and events_logger = "file"

2. Switch back to non-root user. Try running any podman command. For e.g. `podman info`, `podman images`

**Describe the results you received:**
```
testuser@DESKTOP-TESTMACH:~$ podman info
cannot clone: Invalid argument
Error: cannot re-exec process
testuser@DESKTOP-TESTMACH:~$ podman images
cannot clone: Invalid argument
Error: cannot re-exec process
testuser@DESKTOP-TESTMACH:~$ podman ps -a
cannot clone: Invalid argument
Error: cannot re-exec process
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
I expect podman commands to work when run as a non-root user. 

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

podman commands do work when I switch to root user.

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
 This is tested on a Ubuntu 20.04 WSL running inside a Windows 10 VM.

comments.body:
this is working fine for me ... 

```ii  conmon                         2.0.22~1                          amd64        OCI container runtime monitor
ii  cri-o-runc                     1.0.0~rc92.3                      amd64        Open Container Project - runtime
ii  crun                           0.16~2                            amd64        OCI runtime written in C
ii  podman                         2.2.1~4                           amd64        Manage pods, containers and container images.
ii  podman-plugins                 1.1.1~1                           amd64        Plugins for podman
```

one note, i made non of the aforementioned conf file changes
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@rhatdan what is to say, as i said earlier, it worked perfectly for me with a brand new install.
Ok I am going to close, unless we get more information.

I can confirm this behavior on WSL 2 with the Fedora Remix:

Rootfull works (at least info), rootless is throwing that clone exec error.

I did swap the root config to cgroupfs and file as events_backend.

Can we re-open @rhatdan / @baude ?

This is a fresh installation.
It could be that the Kernel is too old since the (managed) Windows Version is quite old.
I encountered same issue, but solved by using WSL2 kernel package, you can have try: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/wsl/install-win10#step-4---download-the-linux-kernel-update-package

Ensure that your WSL Distribution is really at **version 2**.
Mine was installed as version 1 and I had to migrate it to version 2 to get the rootless work :)

```
wsl --list -v
  NAME                   STATE           VERSION
* Ubuntu-20.04           Running         1
wsl --set-version Ubuntu-20.04 2
La conversion est en cours. Cette opération peut prendre quelques minutes...
La conversion est terminée.
wsl --list -v
  NAME                   STATE           VERSION
* Ubuntu-20.04           Running         2
```
Hope that helps.
